description: Configure a ground station in Austin, TX (30.2672 N, 97.7431 W, 149m) as the default. Create a tracking module named 'Deployment_Sim' containing the ISS (ZARYA) (NORAD 25544) and the CSS (TIANHE) (NORAD 48274). Configure the application to display UTC time. Set the simulated time to April 15, 2026, at 14:30:00 with the real-time clock paused, leaving the Time Controller interface open and visible. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

natural_language.prompt: I need to set up GPredict to simulate an upcoming satellite deployment from the space station. First, create a ground station for Austin, TX at 30.2672 North, 97.7431 West, altitude 149m, and make it the default station. Then create a new module called 'Deployment_Sim' and add the ISS (ZARYA, 25544) and CSS (TIANHE, 48274) to it. Go into preferences and make sure we are using UTC time. Finally, open the Time Controller, pause the clock, and fast-forward the simulation to exactly April 15, 2026, at 14:30:00. Please leave the Time Controller window open when you are done so I can review the pass geometry.
